text:
## Application Landscape  
### Core Banking Systems  
#### 1. Account Management System (AMS)  
**System ID:** AMS-001
**Purpose:** Central repository for customer accounts, balances, and transactions
**Technology:** Java microservices, PostgreSQL database
**Deployment:** Cloud (AWS)
**Availability:** 99.9% (SLA)  
**Key Functions:**
- Account creation and lifecycle
- Balance management
- Transaction recording
- Interest calculation
- Account status tracking  
**Data Model:**
- Customers
- Accounts (see [[business_ontology]])
- Account Holders (primary, secondary, joint)
- Transactions
- Account Status  
**APIs Exposed:**
- `POST /accounts` — Create account
- `GET /accounts/{accountId}` — Retrieve account details
- `GET /accounts/{accountId}/balance` — Get balance
- `GET /accounts/{accountId}/transactions` — Transaction history
- `POST /accounts/{accountId}/transactions` — Record transaction  
**Integration:**
- Internet Banking Platform (IBP)
- Mobile Banking App
- Fraud Detection Engine
- Data Warehouse  
**Database:**
- PostgreSQL 13+
- 800 accounts × 2,000+ transactions = 1.6M records
- Backup: Daily encrypted backup to S3
- Recovery Point Objective (RPO): 15 minutes  
---
name: Chunk 4/39

raw_entities.name: Account Management System
raw_entities.description: Serves as the central repository for managing customer accounts, balances, and transactions. Built on Java microservices and PostgreSQL, it operates in a cloud environment (AWS) and exposes RESTful APIs for account lifecycle management, balance retrieval, and transaction recording. It integrates with internet banking, mobile apps, fraud detection, and data warehousing systems.
context: Central repository for customer accounts, balances, and transactions

raw_entities.name: Internet Banking Platform
raw_entities.description: A customer-facing digital channel that integrates with the core account management system to deliver online banking services and facilitate account access through web interfaces.

raw_entities.name: Mobile Banking App
raw_entities.description: A mobile application integrated with the core banking infrastructure to provide customers with account access and transaction capabilities on mobile devices.

raw_entities.name: Fraud Detection Engine
raw_entities.description: A security and compliance system integrated with the account management platform to monitor and identify potentially fraudulent activities across customer accounts and transactions.

raw_entities.name: Data Warehouse
raw_entities.description: A centralized data repository that ingests information from the account management system to support analytical reporting, business intelligence, and historical data analysis.

raw_entities.name: PostgreSQL Database
raw_entities.description: The underlying relational database technology powering the Account Management System, storing core banking data and supporting high-volume transaction processing with defined record scaling.

raw_entities.name: AWS Cloud Infrastructure
raw_entities.description: The cloud hosting environment where the Account Management System and its associated microservices are deployed to ensure scalability, performance, and high availability.

raw_entities.name: System Availability SLA
raw_entities.description: The defined service level agreement specifying the required uptime percentage for the Account Management System to ensure continuous operational access and meet customer expectations.

raw_entities.name: Recovery Point Objective
raw_entities.description: The maximum acceptable amount of data loss measured in time, defining the recovery target for the system's database backups and ensuring minimal data disruption during failover events.

raw_entities.name: Daily Encrypted Backup Schedule
raw_entities.description: A defined data protection frequency and security standard ensuring regular, encrypted preservation of critical banking records to S3 storage for disaster recovery compliance.
context: Backup: Daily encrypted backup to S3

raw_entities.name: Customer
raw_entities.description: A primary party entity within the banking ontology, representing individuals or entities whose accounts and transactions are managed by the core system.

raw_entities.name: Account
raw_entities.description: A core banking product entity that holds balances and records transactions, serving as a fundamental data model component within the account management system.

raw_entities.name: Account Holder
raw_entities.description: A party role associated with an account, defined by primary, secondary, or joint ownership structures within the banking data model.

raw_entities.name: Transaction
raw_entities.description: A financial event recorded within an account, representing the movement or recording of funds that contributes to balance management and historical tracking.

raw_entities.name: Interest Rate
raw_entities.description: A financial parameter calculated and applied to accounts, representing the cost of borrowing or return on deposits as part of account lifecycle management.

raw_entities.name: Account Status
raw_entities.description: A tracking attribute that defines the current operational state or lifecycle phase of a customer account within the banking system.

properties.purpose: Central repository for customer accounts, balances, and transactions

properties.what_it_measures: Maximum acceptable data loss window during recovery

properties.definition: Entities whose financial records are managed by the AMS
Financial record managed by the AMS containing balances and transactions
business_rules: Requires lifecycle management and balance tracking

properties.definition: Entity linked to an account for ownership or access purposes
Record of financial movement linked to an account
business_rules: Must be recorded against a valid account

properties.definition: Rate used for calculating interest on accounts
Current state or phase of an account
business_rules: Must be tracked throughout the account lifecycle

relationships.description: AMS integrates with the Internet Banking Platform to provide account data and transaction services.
AMS integrates with the Mobile Banking App to support mobile account access and transactions.
AMS integrates with the Fraud Detection Engine to enable real-time transaction monitoring and fraud prevention.
AMS feeds account and transaction data to the Data Warehouse for analytics and reporting.
AMS relies on PostgreSQL as its primary relational database for storing account and transaction data.
AMS is deployed on and depends on AWS Cloud Infrastructure for hosting and scalability.
AMS performance and reliability are measured against a 99.9% System Availability SLA.
AMS data loss tolerance is measured by a 15-minute Recovery Point Objective.
AMS data persistence is subject to a daily encrypted backup schedule to S3.
relationships.context: Backup: Daily encrypted backup to S3

relationships.description: AMS stores core customer entity data as part of its central repository function.
AMS manages account lifecycle, balances, and core account attributes.
relationships.context: Key Functions: - Account creation and lifecycle

relationships.description: AMS stores account holder relationships including primary, secondary, and joint structures.
relationships.context: Data Model: - Account Holders (primary, secondary, joint)

relationships.description: AMS processes and records transaction data for all managed accounts.
AMS calculates and applies interest rates to accounts based on configured business rules.
AMS tracks the current status of accounts throughout their lifecycle.
